{"content": "     1|# Slack Org Wiki — LLM Wiki Schema
     2|
     3|Reference inspiration:
     4|- Karpathy LLM Wiki gist: https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f
     5|
     6|## Design stance for this repo
     7|
     8|The Slack-only derived wiki remains **wiki-only**:
     9|- ✅ structured LLM-organized pages
    10|- ✅ derived facts, decisions, actions, open questions
    11|- ✅ channel/time/source metadata
    12|- ❌ raw Slack transcripts
    13|- ❌ verbatim message dumps
    14|- ❌ copied RTS payloads
    15|- ❌ any use of Slack data for model training
    16|
    17|When the unified `company-wiki` pipeline is used, raw Slack snapshots may also
    18|exist under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`, but derived wiki pages still
    19|must not embed raw source bodies directly.
    20|
    21|## Shared contract root
    22|
    23|This lane is governed by the shared docs-root contract under:
    24|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/index.md`
    25|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/log.md`
    26|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/taxonomy.md`
    27|
    28|This file remains the Slack lane schema overlay for that shared contract.
    29|
    30|## Contract enforcement
    31|
    32|The mutation-capable Slack wiki build entrypoint must fail fast when the shared
    33|docs-root contract or this schema file is missing:
    34|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py`
    35|
    36|## Canonical directory layout
    37|
    38|Slack-derived pages now land in the canonical company wiki root:
    39|
    40|```text
    41|workspace/company-wiki/
    42|  SCHEMA.md
    43|  index.md
    44|  log.md
    45|  raw/slack/
    46|  entities/
    47|  concepts/
    48|  comparisons/
    49|  queries/
    50|  _build/
    51|```
    52|
    53|## Structured summary input contract
    54|
    55|`workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py` is now a wrapper into canonical `company-wiki/wiki_build.py`, and expects JSON shaped like either:
    56|
    57|```json
    58|{
    59|  "entries": [ ... ]
    60|}
    61|```
    62|
    63|or a top-level array of entries.
    64|
    65|Each entry should look like:
    66|
    67|```json
    68|{
    69|  "title": "Product priority drift",
    70|  "topic": "product-priority",
    71|  "summary": "Discussion suggests the roadmap is slipping toward reactive support work.",
    72|  "facts": [
    73|    "Multiple channels referenced launch uncertainty",
    74|    "Support load is displacing roadmap work"
    75|  ],
    76|  "decisions": [
    77|    "Need weekly product review"
    78|  ],
    79|  "actions": [
    80|    "Assign owner for launch readiness"
    81|  ],
    82|  "open_questions": [
    83|    "Is this concentrated in one team or org-wide?"
    84|  ],
    85|  "entities": ["product", "support", "launch"],
    86|  "channel_refs": ["C123", "C456"],
    87|  "source_refs": ["admin-inventory:2026-04-09"],
    88|  "time_range": "2026-04-01/2026-04-07",
    89|  "confidence": "medium",
    90|  "updated_at": "2026-04-09T00:00:00Z"
    91|}
    92|```
    93|
    94|## Forbidden raw-content fields
    95|
    96|The builder rejects entries containing keys such as:
    97|- `messages`
    98|- `raw_messages`
    99|- `transcript`
   100|- `raw_text`
   101|- `body`
   102|- `content`
   103|
   104|Keep inputs summarized.
   105|
   106|It also rejects undocumented top-level fields and non-string list payloads, so
   107|structured summaries cannot smuggle nested raw message data into persistent
   108|artifacts.
   109|
   110|## Generated page contract
   111|
   112|Each page contains:
   113|1. title
   114|2. updated timestamp
   115|3. summary
   116|4. key facts
   117|5. decisions
   118|6. actions
   119|7. open questions
   120|8. entities
   121|9. channel/source refs
   122|10. curation note stating that no raw Slack content is stored
   123|
   124|## Retrieval model
   125|
   126|`workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py` is now a wrapper into canonical `company-wiki/wiki_query.py`:
   127|- token-match search over canonical sectioned pages
   128|- returns top matches with snippets
   129|- meant for local assistant grounding, not transcript search
   130|
   131|Future work can add embeddings if the project enables that later.
   132|
   133|## Public-channel-first flow in this workspace
   134|
   135|Current live-access flow:
   136|1. use `recent_public_channels.py` to get public channels with a recent
   137|   metadata update signal
   138|2. let the LLM organize those channels into structured summary JSON
   139|3. run `wiki_build.py`
   140|4. retrieve with `wiki_query.py`
   141|
   142|This is intentionally **LLM-organized**, not a fully automated transcript-ingest
   143|pipeline.
   144|", "total_lines": 143, "file_size": 3759, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — company-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`company-wiki` is the top-level platform-agnostic company knowledge surface.
     6|
     7|It owns:
     8|- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
     9|- Slack + Trello source ingestion into one shared substrate
    10|- deduped raw/manifests under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    11|- derived wiki generation under sectioned directories (`entities/`, `concepts/`, `comparisons/`, `queries/`)
    12|- one unified local query surface
    13|- authoritative `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, `log.md` at the canonical root
    14|
    15|Use this when the user wants:
    16|- one company wiki/search surface instead of Slack/Trello silos
    17|- raw snapshot caching plus AI-generated wiki refresh
    18|- a cron/manual rebuild path for the company wiki
    19|- source-specific retrieval methods documented as one operating workflow
    20|
    21|## Retrieval Methodology
    22|
    23|The company wiki is a **4-stage pipeline**:
    24|
    25|1. **Retrieve** — pull whatever each source can actually expose right now
    26|2. **Persist raw** — save retrievable source payloads under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    27|3. **Normalize** — convert source-specific payloads into one shared derived-entry schema
    28|4. **Build/query** — generate/search derived wiki pages under `workspace/company-wiki/`
    29|
    30|Default retrieval/search always targets the **derived wiki**, not raw payloads.
    31|
    32|## Canonical root contract
    33|
    34|`company-wiki` is governed by the canonical root itself:
    35|- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`
    36|- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`
    37|- `workspace/company-wiki/log.md`
    38|
    39|Reference-only Hermes material may remain under `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/`, but it is not authoritative for active company-wiki flow.
    40|
    41|## Source Adapters
    42|
    43|### Slack adapter
    44|
    45|Current retrieval tiers:
    46|
    47|1. **Workspace public-channel metadata**
    48|   - Uses `conversations.list`
    49|   - Captures public channel inventory, membership flags, and channel `updated` timestamps
    50|   - This is the baseline guaranteed path in this workspace
    51|
    52|2. **Public-channel message history raw**
    53|   - Uses `conversations.history` via the configured bot-token path after public-channel join
    54|   - Current refresh path attempts joins on selected public channels and persists raw history snapshots for channels where access succeeds
    55|   - Initial archive baseline runs use `--archive-mode archive` and persist history in chunked raw records plus per-channel checkpoints for resume
    56|   - Archive mode now also probes broader Slack inventory surfaces (`oversight.conversations.list`, `admin.conversations.search`) **if available** and widens candidate channels when those scopes actually work
    57|   - Hourly cron stays `incremental` only and uses per-channel latest message watermarks rather than replaying baseline pages
    58|   - This is the preferred path when the goal is a real content-backed company wiki refresh rather than metadata-only bootstrap
    59|   - Coverage is still partial and capability-dependent per channel/scope, so manifests must record successes and blockers explicitly
    60|
    61|3. **Private/admin inventory**
    62|   - Only allowed when the exact API path/scopes are verified
    63|   - Must never be implied from CLI login alone
    64|
    65|Excluded:
    66|- DM / 1:1
    67|- any unverified private-channel message visibility
    68|
    69|### Trello adapter
    70|
    71|Current retrieval order:
    72|
    73|1. `members/me` — auth/identity sanity check
    74|2. `members/me/organizations` — visible workspace/org list
    75|3. `members/me/boards` — visible board inventory
    76|4. `boards/<id>` — board detail
    77|5. `boards/<id>/members` — board member coverage
    78|6. `boards/<id>/cards` — board card snapshot
    79|
    80|Trello remains strictly **read-only**.
    81|
    82|## Raw Snapshot Contract
    83|
    84|Raw payloads live under:
    85|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`
    86|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
    87|
    88|Each source keeps:
    89|- `items/` — deduped raw payload records
    90|- `dedupe-index.json` — payload fingerprint index
    91|- `runs/manifest-*.json` — per-run source manifest
    92|- `channel-checkpoints/*.json` — per-channel archive/incremental resume state for Slack history capture
    93|
    94|Top-level run manifests live under:
    95|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`
    96|
    97|Rules:
    98|- raw is source-labeled
    99|- raw is snapshot-based, not event-sourced
   100|- repeated identical payloads should dedupe rather than multiply
   101|- Slack history payloads are persisted as chunked `channel_history_chunk` raw items rather than one large per-channel blob
   102|- raw may be incomplete if source capability is incomplete; blockers must be recorded explicitly
   103|
   104|## Normalize Contract
   105|
   106|Normalized entries are derived from raw snapshots and must preserve:
   107|- `source_type`
   108|- `access_scope`
   109|- `source_refs`
   110|- `raw_manifest_ref`
   111|- factual summary fields (`facts`, `decisions`, `actions`, `open_questions`, etc.)
   112|
   113|What normalization does:
   114|- maps source-specific payloads into one shared wiki-entry shape
   115|- preserves provenance and caveats
   116|- avoids leaking raw bodies into derived page content
   117|
   118|## Query Semantics
   119|
   120|Default query path:
   121|
   122|```bash
   123|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   124|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   125|  --query "product risk"
   126|```
   127|
   128|Query semantics:
   129|- searches Hermes-style derived sections:
   130|  - `entities/`
   131|  - `concepts/`
   132|  - `comparisons/`
   133|  - `queries/`
   134|- does not search raw payload bodies by default
   135|- returns mixed-source results in one surface
   136|- source provenance is preserved inside the page content/refs
   137|
   138|Interpretation rules:
   139|- a Slack page reflects what was retrievable from Slack at refresh time
   140|- a Trello page reflects current-state Trello snapshots at refresh time
   141|- mixed-source hits should be read as one company-memory surface, not as identical-confidence evidence
   142|
   143|## Refresh Runbook
   144|
   145|### Prepare Slack bot membership when richer history retrieval is needed
   146|
   147|```bash
   148|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   149|```
   150|
   151|### Full refresh
   152|
   153|```bash
   154|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py --slack-team moveis
   155|```
   156|
   157|### Initial public-channel archive baseline
   158|
   159|```bash
   160|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   161|  --slack-team moveis \
   162|  --archive-mode archive \
   163|  --slack-channel-limit 50 \
   164|  --slack-history-limit 200 \
   165|  --slack-history-chunk-size 100
   166|```
   167|
   168|This mode:
   169|1. joins the current high-priority public channels visible to the bot token
   170|2. probes broader workspace/admin inventory if those scopes are actually available
   171|3. captures as much channel history as the current access path can actually read
   172|4. stores history as chunked raw records
   173|4. writes/updates `raw/slack/channel-checkpoints/*.json`
   174|5. resumes incomplete baseline channels on later archive runs via saved Slack cursors
   175|
   176|Honest scope note:
   177|- if admin/oversight scopes are unavailable, archive mode falls back to the public-channel, bot-accessible path and records that limitation explicitly in raw manifests
   178|
   179|### Hourly incremental refresh (cron-safe default)
   180|
   181|```bash
   182|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   183|  --slack-team moveis \
   184|  --archive-mode incremental
   185|```
   186|
   187|What it does:
   188|1. captures accessible Slack source payloads
   189|2. captures accessible Trello source payloads
   190|3. writes raw source manifests
   191|4. writes a top-level run manifest
   192|5. rebuilds derived wiki pages
   193|
   194|Incremental mode uses per-channel latest-message checkpoints/watermarks and remains the only mode wired into the hourly cron wrapper by default.
   195|
   196|### Query after refresh
   197|
   198|```bash
   199|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   200|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   201|  --query "slack trello dev"
   202|```
   203|
   204|### Cron/manual wrapper
   205|
   206|```bash
   207|workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh
   208|```
   209|
   210|Operational reality in this workspace:
   211|- The canonical wiki root is `workspace/company-wiki/`, but the current wrapper scripts are still anchored to `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`.
   212|- Treat this as a migration-era layout: Hermes-style wiki structure on top of a ZeroClaw-rooted runtime path.
   213|- When auditing readiness, check both Hermes cron state and system crontab state; an empty Hermes cron list does not mean the refresh is unscheduled.
   214|
   215|Cron invariant:
   216|- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` installs the refresh into system crontab in `incremental` mode by default, currently at minute 17 of every hour unless overridden by `COMPANY_WIKI_CRON_SCHEDULE`.
   217|- Use `COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=archive` only for an intentional one-off/manual baseline catch-up, not the hourly cron loop.
   218|- Invoke the installer as a shell script (`bash workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` or direct execute). Do not run `.sh` installers with `python3`.
   219|
   220|Optional cron installers:
   221|
   222|```bash
   223|workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
   224|workspace/bin/uninstall_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
   225|```
   226|
   227|## Failure Modes / Degrade Strategy
   228|
   229|- If one source is blocked, the refresh should still complete for other sources.
   230|- The blocked source must be marked in source manifests and the top-level run manifest.
   231|- Missing Slack history scope is a **capability limit**, not a hard failure.
   232|- Missing Trello credentials is a real blocker and must surface clearly.
   233|
   234|## Verification Checklist
   235|
   236|Minimum acceptable verification after meaningful changes:
   237|
   238|1. `python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py --slack-team moveis`
   239|2. `python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py --root workspace/company-wiki --query "..."`
   240|3. `python3 -m unittest workspace/test/company_wiki_pipeline_test.py workspace/test/slack_org_wiki_guardrails_test.py`
   241|4. `node --test workspace/test/slack_org_wiki_build.test.js`
   242|5. live capability checks when relevant:
   243|   - Slack `conversations.list`
   244|   - Slack `conversations.history` probe
   245|   - Trello `/members/me`
   246|   - Trello `/members/me/boards`
   247|
   248|## Output Contract
   249|
   250|1. **Source refresh status**
   251|2. **Run manifest path**
   252|3. **Derived wiki build status**
   253|4. **Query findings**
   254|5. **Blocked-source caveats**
   255|
   256|## Guardrails
   257|
   258|- Raw source data may be persisted only under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`.
   259|- Default wiki retrieval must return derived pages, not raw payloads.
   260|- Do not imply private-channel or DM visibility without verified access.
   261|- Do not imply Trello write authority.
   262|- Do not treat Slack metadata access as message-history access.
   263|", "total_lines": 262, "file_size": 9992, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — github-org-intel
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`github-org-intel` is the workspace-local GitHub portfolio intelligence surface.
     6|Use it when the user wants **read-only `gh` CLI analysis** of the internal org,
     7|repo/module health, cautious contributor/account signals, or management action
     8|items derived from GitHub state.
     9|
    10|This is a **workspace skill**, not a global Codex skill.
    11|
    12|## When to Use
    13|
    14|Route here for prompts like:
    15|
    16|- “Give me a GitHub portfolio snapshot for orbisoptimus.”
    17|- “Which repos are active vs stale right now?”
    18|- “Analyze `orbisoptimus/orbi_app` and tell me the repo health.”
    19|- “Give me a cautious contributor summary for bellman-move.”
    20|- “Turn current GitHub state into action items for product, engineering, and AI.”
    21|
    22|If the question is really about Orbi community/user behavior, route to Orbi
    23|surfaces first. Use this skill for **software portfolio / delivery / backlog /
    24|branch hygiene / contributor-signal** questions.
    25|
    26|## Core Rules
    27|
    28|- `gh` CLI first.
    29|- Read-only only.
    30|- No GitHub write actions.
    31|- No HR/performance scoring from GitHub traces.
    32|- Account analysis is opt-in and must include confidence + visibility caveats.
    33|- Prefer repo/stream-level judgment over person-level inference.
    34|- If auth or visibility is incomplete, say so explicitly.
    35|
    36|## Local Helper Scripts
    37|
    38|Use these workspace-local helpers:
    39|
    40|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py`
    41|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/repo_deep_dive.py`
    42|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/account_summary.py`
    43|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/render_report.py`
    44|
    45|Reference docs:
    46|
    47|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/privacy-and-confidence.md`
    48|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/orbi-stream-mapping.md`
    49|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/output-templates.md`
    50|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/metric-definitions.md`
    51|
    52|## Workflows
    53|
    54|### 1. Portfolio snapshot
    55|
    56|Use when the user wants org posture.
    57|
    58|```bash
    59|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py --org orbisoptimus --format markdown
    60|```
    61|
    62|Output contract:
    63|- scope / timestamp / auth context
    64|- active repos summary
    65|- stale / archived summary
    66|- backlog hotspots
    67|- branch hygiene notes
    68|- Orbi stream mapping
    69|- priority actions
    70|- confidence / limitation notes
    71|
    72|### 2. Repo / module deep dive
    73|
    74|Use when the user names a repo.
    75|
    76|```bash
    77|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/repo_deep_dive.py --repo orbisoptimus/orbi_app --format markdown
    78|```
    79|
    80|Output contract:
    81|- repo identity + default branch
    82|- recent activity summary
    83|- issue / PR backlog summary
    84|- module-performance view
    85|- risks / unknowns
    86|- recommended next actions
    87|- confidence / limitation notes
    88|
    89|### 3. Account / contributor view
    90|
    91|Use only when the user explicitly names a target account.
    92|
    93|```bash
    94|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/account_summary.py --org orbisoptimus --account bellman-move --format markdown
    95|```
    96|
    97|Output contract:
    98|- target account
    99|- visible evidence summary
   100|- repo / PR / issue / review signals
   101|- confidence level
   102|- visibility caveats
   103|- non-evaluative note

   105|
   106|### 4. Management action memo

   112|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py --org orbisoptimus --format json > "$tmp"

   115|```
   116|
   117|Output contract:

   121|- confidence / limitation notes
   122|
   123|## Orbi-specific framing
   124|

   134|- what leadership should do next
   135|
   136|## Guardrails
   137|
   138|- Do not imply direct GitHub mutation authority in this workflow.
   139|- Do not turn GitHub activity into compensation/promotion judgments.
   140|- Treat visibility-limited people analysis as low-confidence or insufficient visibility.
   141|- For internal/private repo reporting, summarize before exposing unnecessary detail.

{"content": "     1|# Skill — slack-org-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`slack-org-wiki` is the workspace-local Slack source lane for **official Slack CLI auth**, **Enterprise admin conversation inventory**, and **Slack ingestion into local wiki surfaces**.
     6|
     7|Use it when the user wants to:
     8|- bootstrap Slack admin access via the official Slack CLI
     9|- inspect org-wide accessible channels/conversations
    10|- turn recent Slack findings into an LLM-maintained company wiki
    11|- keep the current bot runtime while adding a separate admin/control lane
    12|
    13|## When to Use
    14|
    15|Route here for prompts like:
    16|- “Log me into Slack CLI and use my admin access.”
    17|- “Inventory every accessible Slack channel for this org.”
    18|- “Turn recent Slack chatter into an LLM wiki.”
    19|- “Use the official Slack CLI path, not pasted tokens.”
    20|- “Build a company memory/wiki from Slack without storing raw messages.”
    21|
    22|If the request is only about querying already-built wiki pages, prefer `company-wiki` or `slack-llm-wiki-retrieval`, both of which now route to the canonical `workspace/company-wiki/` surface.
    23|
    24|## Core Rules
    25|
    26|- Default to the assistant's configured Slack token/permissions when describing or executing Slack access in this workspace.
    27|- Treat Slack CLI user-auth as a deprecated/legacy path, not the default operating story.
    28|- Preserve the existing Socket Mode bot/runtime.
    29|- Do not paste auth tickets, challenge codes, or tokens into chat.
    30|- Do not put raw Slack transcripts, raw message bodies, or raw file bodies into derived wiki pages.
    31|- Raw Slack snapshots are allowed only under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` when using the unified `company-wiki` pipeline.
    32|- Distinguish clearly between:
    33|  1. CLI auth/bootstrap
    34|  2. conversation metadata inventory
    35|  3. ephemeral search/review
    36|  4. persistent LLM-wiki storage
    37|- Do not claim private-channel message visibility unless the specific Slack API path and permissions are actually available.
    38|- Treat Real-time Search results as non-persistable.
    39|- Require proper internal disclosure/consent/privacy review before storing derived Slack knowledge.
    40|- Do not position this workflow as covert surveillance; align it with legitimate admin/compliance/internal-knowledge use.
    41|
    42|## Local Helper Scripts
    43|
    44|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py`
    45|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py`
    46|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py`
    47|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py`
    48|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py`
    49|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py`
    50|
    51|Reference docs:
    52|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/official-notes.md`
    53|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/llm-wiki-schema.md`
    54|
    55|## Workflows
    56|
    57|### 1. Confirm local Slack CLI/auth state
    58|
    59|```bash
    60|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py --format markdown
    61|```
    62|
    63|Use this first to confirm:
    64|- `slack` exists on PATH
    65|- local CLI version
    66|- `slack auth list` status
    67|- whether `~/.slack/credentials.json` exists
    68|- whether CLI credential entries and/or a service/admin token env var are present
    69|
    70|### 2. Inventory accessible conversations (metadata only)
    71|
    72|```bash
    73|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    74|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
    75|  --method conversations-list \
    76|  --channel-type public_channel \
    77|  --exclude-archived \
    78|  --format markdown
    79|```
    80|
    81|for the current directly accessible public-channel surface, or:
    82|
    83|```bash
    84|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    85|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
    86|  --method oversight \
    87|  --format markdown
    88|```
    89|
    90|or, with an explicit token:
    91|
    92|```bash
    93|SLACK_ADMIN_TOKEN=*** \
    94|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    95|  --method oversight \
    96|  --format markdown
    97|```
    98|
    99|Use this for org/workspace conversation cataloging. Output is metadata-only.
   100|
   101|### 3. Join visible public channels with the oracle bot when history access is needed
   102|
   103|```bash
   104|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   105|```
   106|
   107|Use this when the bot token has history scopes but is not yet a member of target public channels. Joining may convert `not_in_channel` into usable history access.
   108|
   109|### 4. Build Slack-derived pages into the canonical company wiki
   110|
   111|```bash
   112|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py \
   113|  --input workspace/tmp/slack-summary.json \
   114|  --root workspace/company-wiki
   115|```
   116|
   117|Input must already be a **structured summary artifact**. Do not feed raw transcripts.
   118|
   119|### 5. Pull active public-channel candidates for manual/LLM summarization
   120|
   121|```bash
   122|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py \
   123|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
   124|  --days 7 \
   125|  --format markdown
   126|```
   127|
   128|This is a **channel-activity heuristic** based on channel metadata, not proof of recent message reads.
   129|
   130|### 6. Query the canonical company wiki
   131|
   132|```bash
   133|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   134|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   135|  --query "product risk"
   136|```
   137|
   138|## Output Contract
   139|
   140|Default structure:
   141|1. **Auth / Access State**
   142|2. **Inventory Findings**
   143|3. **Wiki Findings**
   144|4. **Caveats / Permission Limits**
   145|5. **Next Actions**
   146|
   147|## Guardrails
   148|
   149|- Never store raw Slack messages in generated wiki artifacts.
   150|- If raw Slack data is persisted for the unified company-wiki pipeline, keep it under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` with run-manifest visibility.
   151|- Slack wiki build/query wrappers are canonical-root adapters; they must not create a parallel Slack-only wiki root.
   152|- Never imply that Slack CLI auth alone grants unrestricted private-channel message access.
   153|- Prefer metadata inventory plus structured summaries over bulk collection.
   154|- Current public-channel “active in last 7 days” flow is heuristic unless `channels:history` or `search:read` is added later.
   155|- If the required admin/oversight scopes are missing, say so explicitly.
   156|", "total_lines": 155, "file_size": 5793, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — create_slack_cron
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`create_slack_cron` creates **reliable Slack-delivery cron automations** using a
     6|shell wrapper instead of agent cron `delivery.announce`.
     7|
     8|Use this when the output must actually land in Slack on schedule and the safer
     9|path is:
    10|
    11|1. shell cron starts
    12|2. local script runs `zeroclaw agent -m "<prompt>"`
    13|3. output is saved to disk
    14|4. `zeroclaw channel send` posts it to Slack
    15|
    16|## When to Use
    17|
    18|Use for prompts like:
    19|
    20|- “슬랙으로 가는 크론 만들어줘”
    21|- “매일/매주 보고를 Slack에 자동 발송해줘”
    22|- “cron delivery 말고 직접 Slack 보내는 방식으로 붙여줘”
    23|- “문제 있는 announce cron 말고 우회 경로로 자동화해줘”
    24|
    25|## Standard Build Pattern
    26|
    27|For each Slack cron, create:
    28|
    29|1. `workspace/prompts/<name>.prompt.txt`
    30|2. `workspace/bin/send_<name>.sh`
    31|3. a shell cron job that calls that script
    32|
    33|## Script Contract
    34|
    35|The shell script should usually:
    36|
    37|1. set `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`
    38|2. read a prompt file
    39|3. run:
    40|   - `cd "$ROOT" && RUST_LOG=error zeroclaw agent -m "$prompt"`
    41|4. save stdout to:
    42|   - `workspace/reports/` for report-style outputs
    43|   - `workspace/alerts/` for alert-style outputs
    44|5. save stderr to `workspace/logs/`
    45|6. send stdout with:
    46|   - `zeroclaw channel send "$text" --channel-id slack --recipient <CHANNEL_ID>`
    47|
    48|## Naming Convention
    49|
    50|- Prompt file: `workspace/prompts/<slug>.prompt.txt`
    51|- Script file: `workspace/bin/send_<slug>.sh`
    52|- Report file: `workspace/reports/<slug>-<date>.md`
    53|- Alert file: `workspace/alerts/<slug>-<timestamp>.md`
    54|
    55|## Cron Convention
    56|
    57|- Prefer **shell cron** over agent cron for Slack delivery.
    58|- If an older agent cron already exists for the same purpose, pause it.
    59|- Default timezone to `UTC` unless the user requests another timezone.
    60|- After creation, test the script once manually if safe.
    61|
    62|## Output Checklist
    63|
    64|When finishing, report:
    65|
    66|1. created prompt path
    67|2. created script path
    68|3. cron job id
    69|4. schedule + timezone
    70|5. whether a live send test succeeded
    71|
    72|## Guardrails
    73|
    74|- Do not claim Slack delivery is working unless `zeroclaw channel send` or the
    75|  wrapper script was actually tested successfully.
    76|- Keep prompt text outside the shell script when possible.
    77|- Prefer concise, operator-usable Slack outputs.
    78|", "total_lines": 77, "file_size": 2268, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — trello-wiki-exec-report
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`trello-wiki-exec-report` is the **top-level/default/user-facing Trello-wiki surface**.
     6|Use it when the question should be answered from the built company wiki’s Trello layer rather than live Trello REST alone.
     7|
     8|It is for prompts like:
     9|- “위키 기준으로 Trello 쪽 지금 뭐가 중요해?”
    10|- “Trello 관련 exec 보고로 요약해줘.”
    11|- “이미 구축된 회사 위키에서 Trello 쪽만 읽고 임원용으로 정리해.”
    12|- “raw/derived를 다 감안해서 지금 상태를 한 장으로 설명해.”
    13|
    14|## Scope
    15|
    16|This lane assumes the canonical root is:
    17|- `workspace/company-wiki/`
    18|
    19|And that Trello-related evidence may exist in two layers:
    20|1. raw snapshots under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
    21|2. derived wiki pages under the canonical derived sections
    22|
    23|## Delegation Model
    24|
    25|Use subordinate/internal lanes in this order when needed:
    26|
    27|1. `trello-wiki-raw-read` — inspect raw run manifests, item inventory, and collection caveats
    28|2. `trello-wiki-derived-read` — inspect derived wiki pages/query hits for Trello material
    29|3. `trello-wiki-synthesize` — merge raw collection reality + derived meaning into an executive judgment
    30|
    31|## Workflow
    32|
    33|1. Clarify whether the user wants a wiki-grounded answer rather than live REST-only retrieval.
    34|2. Check Trello raw freshness / coverage / blockers.
    35|3. Check Trello-derived pages and query hits.
    36|4. Reconcile raw-vs-derived gaps before making strong claims.
    37|5. Produce an executive-ready report with implication first.
    38|
    39|## Output Contract
    40|
    41|Default structure:
    42|
    43|1. **Executive Readout** — what matters now
    44|2. **What the Wiki Actually Covers** — raw/derived coverage and freshness
    45|3. **Trello Signals** — boards, work themes, owners, bottlenecks, or drift seen in the wiki
    46|4. **Confidence / Gaps** — where the wiki is strong vs thin
    47|5. **Recommended Next Attention** — what an operator/executive should inspect next
    48|
    49|## Guardrails
    50|
    51|- This lane is wiki-grounded, not live-Trello-first.
    52|- Distinguish raw snapshot fact, derived summary, and final inference.
    53|- Do not imply Trello write authority.
    54|- If the wiki is stale or sparse, say so explicitly.
    55|", "total_lines": 54, "file_size": 2174, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/bin/zsh
     2|set -euo pipefail
     3|
     4|ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"
     5|LOG="$ROOT/workspace/logs/company-wiki-refresh-cron.log"
     6|SCHEDULE="${COMPANY_WIKI_CRON_SCHEDULE:-17 * * * *}"
     7|TEAM="${COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM:-moveis}"
     8|ARCHIVE_MODE="${COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE:-incremental}"
     9|LINE="$SCHEDULE COMPANY_WIKI_SLACK_TEAM=$TEAM COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=$ARCHIVE_MODE $ROOT/workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh >> $LOG 2>&1"
    10|
    11|TMP=$(mktemp)
    12|trap 'rm -f "$TMP"' EXIT
    13|
    14|crontab -l > "$TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
    15|if ! grep -Fq "$ROOT/workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh" "$TMP"; then
    16|  echo "$LINE" >> "$TMP"
    17|fi
    18|crontab "$TMP"
    19|crontab -l
    20|", "total_lines": 19, "file_size": 630, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

     7|
     8|cd "$ROOT"
     9|PYTHONPATH="$ROOT/workspace/bin/company-wiki${PYTHONPATH:+:$PYTHONPATH}" \

    19|---
    20|
    21|# 2026-04-13 company wiki realignment and urgent actions
    22|
    23|## Situation
    24|현재 canonical company wiki는 살아는 있지만, **리더십이 바로 읽는 운영 위키**라기보다 **수집 결과를 나열한 저장소**에 가깝다.
    25|
    26|근거:
    27|- 현재 인덱스는 총 14페이지이며 `entities 11 / concepts 3 / comparisons 0 / queries 0` 구조다.
    28|- 최신 refresh는 `2026-04-13T09:19:42Z`, build는 `2026-04-13T09:20:09Z`에 성공했다.
    29|- Slack 쪽은 최신 refresh에서 public channels 7개 join 성공, history capture 7/7 성공이다.
    30|- Trello 쪽은 open boards 4개가 반영돼 있다.
    31|- 그런데 canonical surface 안에는 **GitHub 운영면이 아직 없다.** 반면 운영 라우팅 문서는 대표 질문에 대해 `company-wiki + GitHub + Trello` 결합을 사실상 표준으로 잡고 있다.
    32|
    33|## Diagnosis
    34|### 1) 정보구조가 운영 우선순위 기준으로 정렬돼 있지 않다
    35|첫 화면이 사람/보드/채널 엔티티 나열 중심이다. 대표나 운영 리드가 보고 싶은 건
    36|1. 지금 뭐가 움직이는지
    37|2. 뭐가 막히는지
    38|3. 오늘 당장 뭘 해야 하는지
    39|인데, 그 레이어가 비어 있다.
    40|
    41|### 2) Slack 파트는 수집 성공 대비 요약 승격이 안 됐다
    42|최신 manifest는 Slack history capture 성공(7/7)을 말하는데, public-channel bootstrap 계열 페이지 8개는 아직 `updated: 2026-04-13T05:07:53Z`에 머물러 있다. 즉 **수집은 더 앞서가는데 위키 서술이 못 따라오고 있다.**
    43|
    44|### 3) 위키 hygiene가 아직 migration-era 흔적을 품고 있다
    45|- canonical pages 16개에 `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw` 절대경로가 남아 있다.
    46|- entity pages 11개가 frontmatter에 `type: entitie` 오타를 가진다.
    47|- log도 절대경로 기반 기록이 많다.
    48|이건 검색/후속 자동화/이식성에서 계속 발목 잡는다.
    49|
    50|### 4) Trello는 운영 신호가 보이는데 우선순위 해석 레이어가 비어 있다
    51|현재 보이는 보드 상태:
    52|- `Dev`: 40 cards, last activity `2026-04-13T05:21:49Z`
    53|- `Design`: 258 cards, last activity `2026-04-07T01:03:03Z`
    54|- `공지`: 19 cards, last activity `2026-04-12T22:02:46Z`
    55|- `잡동사니_아무거나`: 17 cards, last activity `2025-11-17T01:25:00Z`
    56|
    57|의미는 명확하다:
    58|- Dev는 지금 움직이는 실무판이다.
    59|- Design은 카드량 대비 최근 활동이 약해서 **백로그 압력** 가능성이 높다.
    60|- 잡동사니는 사실상 **정리/아카이브 후보**다.
    61|하지만 위키는 아직 이걸 경영 판단으로 압축하지 않는다.
    62|
    63|### 5) “모든 소스” 기준으로는 아직 GitHub가 빠져 있다
    64|GitHub live snapshot에서 최근 업데이트 repo 상위는 다음 흐름을 보여준다.
    65|- `orbisoptimus/orbi-oracle-bot`
    66|- `orbisoptimus/ai-math`
    67|- `orbisoptimus/tutor`
    68|- `orbisoptimus/orbi-viewer-mcp`
    69|- `orbisoptimus/spica`
    70|
    71|즉 현재 회사 실행축은 적어도
    72|- AI/assistant
    73|- tutor/education
    74|- core product/app
    75|으로 보이는데, canonical wiki는 아직 Slack+Trello까지만 묶는다.
    76|
    77|## Recommended reordering
    78|canonical wiki 첫 화면과 읽는 순서를 아래처럼 바꾸는 게 맞다.
    79|
    80|### A. Executive layer
    81|1. `queries/this-week-priorities.md`
    82|2. `queries/blocked-or-stale-work.md`
    83|3. `queries/company-wiki-realignment-and-actions.md`
    84|
    85|### B. Stream layer
    86|1. AI / assistant
    87|2. Growth / ads
    88|3. Product / tutor
    89|4. Release / deploy
    90|
    91|### C. Source layer
    92|1. GitHub execution
    93|2. Trello planning
    94|3. Slack operating signal
    95|
    96|### D. Raw/entity layer
    97|- channels
    98|- boards
    99|- reference concepts
   100|
   101|핵심은 **엔티티 중심 → 의사결정 중심** 전환이다.
   102|
   103|## Urgent action items
   104|### P0
   105|1. **GitHub를 canonical company wiki에 합류시켜라.**  
   106|   지금 구조는 “company wiki”라고 부르지만 실제론 Slack+Trello 위키다. 대표 관점 운영 위키라면 GitHub execution surface가 들어와야 한다.
   107|2. **Slack latest refresh를 queries/action memo로 승격해라.**  
   108|   7개 채널 history를 읽고도 action page가 0개인 상태는 낭비다.
   109|3. **generator hygiene 바로 잡아라.**  
   110|   `type: entitie` 오타, 절대경로 노출, stale updated timestamps를 이번 사이클에서 정리해야 한다.
   111|
   112|### P1
   113|4. **Design board를 backlog-pressure board로 별도 추적해라.**  
   114|   카드 258장 대비 최근 활동이 약하다. owner/active lists/stale cards 기준으로 한번 더 압축해야 한다.
   115|5. **`잡동사니_아무거나`는 archive-or-cleanup 판정 내려라.**  
   116|   2025-11-17 이후 정지면 운영 위키 첫 화면에 남겨둘 자산이 아니다.
   117|6. **`#서비스-배포-알림`을 운영 핵심 채널로 승격해라.**  
   118|   22명 규모고 release/deploy 성격이 명확하다. 운영 사고/배포 cadence 시그널의 우선 채널이다.
   119|
   120|### P2
   121|7. **comparisons/queries를 자동 생성하게 바꿔라.**  
   122|   지금 `comparisons 0 / queries 0`는 위키가 생각을 안 하고 있다는 뜻이다.
   123|8. **index를 source-first가 아니라 action-first로 재작성해라.**
   124|
   125|## Concrete operating view
   126|지금 위키 기준으로 당장 보는 게 맞는 건 세 가지다.
   127|- **실행축:** GitHub 상단 활성 repo 흐름은 AI/assistant + tutor/product 쪽이다.
   128|- **운영축:** Slack은 deploy, ads, AI, tutor 관련 public signal이 잡힌다.
   129|- **계획축:** Trello는 Dev가 현재 실행판, Design은 과적 가능성, 잡동사니는 정리 후보다.
   130|
   131|## Bottom line
   132|이 위키는 **수집 체계는 붙었고, 경영 위키로의 마지막 20%가 비어 있다.**
   133|지금 급한 건 데이터를 더 모으는 게 아니라,
   134|1. GitHub를 canonical surface에 넣고,
   135|2. Slack/Trello 결과를 action memo로 승격하고,
   136|3. stale/hygiene 문제를 치우는 것이다.
   137|", "total_lines": 136, "file_size": 6559, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

**Restructuring resources**

I need to reorganize, starting with updating the SCHEMA and index. The user's request to reorder the initial wiki suggests some changes in the documentation. Since they mentioned "using all resources", I should create resource-focused pages from Slack, Trello, GitHub, Orbi, and skills/cron. Maybe I’ll need to write new markdown files and pull from actual sources like the GitHub repo and Orbi results. I could also add a concept page for a "cross-source operating surfaces overview".
**Reorganizing the initial wiki**

The user has asked for a reorganization of the initial wiki rather than a complete overhaul. I should inspect one entity page's structure to see if there’s a way to patch type lines across multiple files. Since there are 11 files involved, I could use patch mode to manage the updates more efficiently. It might be simpler to create a Python script for programmatic patches. {"content": "     1|# Shared LLM Wiki Taxonomy
     2|
     3|## Page classes
     4|
     5|- `entity` — durable subject pages for systems, teams, repos, tools, channels, or boards
     6|- `concept` — durable process or policy pages
     7|- `comparison` — side-by-side evaluations or tradeoff pages
     8|- `query` — query/result pages or working synthesis pages worth preserving
     9|
    10|## Cross-lane rules
    11|
    12|- Derived pages must keep provenance refs to raw/source artifacts.
    13|- Raw source bodies stay in raw/source roots, not in shared contract docs.
    14|- Shared taxonomy does not imply shared storage.
    15|", "total_lines": 14, "file_size": 536, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Slack Org Wiki — Official Notes
     2|
     3|This note captures the official-platform constraints used for the local Slack org/wiki tooling.
     4|
     5|## Local verification in this workspace
     6|
     7|- On **2026-04-09 UTC**, the official Slack CLI was installed locally.
     8|- `slack version` returned **v3.15.0**.
     9|- `slack auth list` currently reports no logged-in Slack accounts on this machine.
    10|
    11|## Official Slack CLI auth path
    12|
    13|### User login
    14|
    15|Official docs say to authenticate with:
    16|
    17|```bash
    18|slack login
    19|```
    20|
    21|This prints a `/slackauthticket ...` slash command, asks for a challenge code, and saves authorization data to:
    22|
    23|- `~/.slack/credentials.json`
    24|
    25|Useful follow-up:
    26|
    27|```bash
    28|slack auth list
    29|```
    30|
    31|The local helper scripts can also read `~/.slack/credentials.json` directly for
    32|user-authenticated calls. If multiple teams are logged in, pass a selector such
    33|as `--credential-team T123...` or `--credential-team my-team-domain`.
    34|
    35|In this workspace, CLI login has now been completed for:
    36|- `moveis` / `T02QX3SCK`
    37|
    38|### Service token flow
    39|
    40|Official docs also document:
    41|
    42|```bash
    43|slack auth token
    44|```
    45|
    46|That flow returns a long-lived service token, but Slack says it is **not saved** to `credentials.json`; it must be stored securely by the operator.
    47|
    48|Recommended local env vars for the helper scripts:
    49|- `SLACK_ADMIN_TOKEN`
    50|- `SLACK_SERVICE_TOKEN`
    51|
    52|## Enterprise org / private-channel facts
    53|
    54|### Admin channel search
    55|
    56|`admin.conversations.search`:
    57|- is Enterprise-only
    58|- searches public/private channels across an org
    59|- requires an org-installed app and `admin.conversations:read`
    60|- requires the installing Org Admin/Owner to have Channel Management permission, including private-channel access
    61|
    62|This is suitable for **channel discovery / inventory**, not proof of message-body access.
    63|
    64|Current live probe in this workspace:
    65|- `admin.conversations.search` with the current CLI credential returns `missing_scope`
    66|
    67|### Oversight inventory
    68|
    69|`oversight.conversations.list`:
    70|- is part of the Admin Oversight API
    71|- can return a paginated list of conversations across an Enterprise org
    72|- exposes truncated metadata
    73|- is a better fit for inventory/catalog use than bulk message export
    74|
    75|Slack says the Admin Oversight API is **not intended for bulk message collection**.
    76|
    77|Current live probe in this workspace:
    78|- `oversight.conversations.list` with the current CLI credential returns `missing_scope`
    79|
    80|### Default Slack search visibility
    81|
    82|Slack Help says private-channel messages/files appear in search results only for people in the channel.
    83|
    84|That means ordinary membership/search behavior is narrower than org-admin channel-management visibility.
    85|
    86|## Real-time Search caveat
    87|
    88|Slack’s Real-time Search API explicitly says:
    89|- apps may use it with zero-data-copy semantics
    90|- apps **must not store or copy** retrieved data
    91|- apps may not use it to scrape unrelated workspace data
    92|
    93|Because of that, the local LLM-wiki tooling does **not** treat RTS as a persistent-storage source.
    94|
    95|## Policy / compliance note
    96|
    97|Slack's Developer Policy says apps must not collect/store/use Data without proper consent, must not use Slack Data to train an LLM, and must not access Data for surveillance purposes.
    98|
    99|For this repo, that means:
   100|- no model-training on Slack data
   101|- no covert or undisclosed capture
   102|- explicit privacy/disclosure/legal review before org-wide rollout
   103|- prefer aggregated/admin/compliance/internal-knowledge use that is documented to users
   104|
   105|## Practical implication for this repo
   106|
   107|The repo’s new Slack org/wiki lane is intentionally split into:
   108|1. **official CLI auth/bootstrap**
   109|2. **metadata inventory** (`conversations.list` for directly accessible channels, admin APIs when granted)
   110|3. **structured-summary-to-wiki build**
   111|4. **local wiki retrieval**
   112|
   113|It does **not** claim that Slack CLI auth alone unlocks unrestricted private-channel transcript ingestion.
   114|
   115|## Official sources
   116|
   117|- Slack CLI docs: https://docs.slack.dev/tools/slack-cli/
   118|- Authorizing the Slack CLI: https://docs.slack.dev/tools/slack-cli/guides/authorizing-the-slack-cli/
   119|- `admin.conversations.search`: https://docs.slack.dev/reference/methods/admin.conversations.search/
   120|- Admin Oversight API: https://docs.slack.dev/admins/admin-oversight-api/
   121|- Real-time Search API: https://docs.slack.dev/apis/web-api/real-time-search-api/
   122|- Slack Help — channel visibility/search: https://slack.com/help/articles/360017938993-What-is-a-channel
   123|- Slack Help — channel management tools: https://slack.com/help/articles/360047512554-Use-channel-management-tools-Use-channel-management-tools
   124|- Slack Developer Policy: https://docs.slack.dev/developer-policy/
   125|", "total_lines": 124, "file_size": 4592, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import argparse
     5|import json
     6|import shutil
     7|from collections import defaultdict
     8|from pathlib import Path
     9|from typing import Any
    10|
    11|from _common import (
    12|    COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT,
    13|    CompanyWikiError,
    14|    append_contract_log,
    15|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root,
    16|    compact_unique,
    17|    company_wiki_paths,
    18|    ensure_contract_ready,
    19|    ensure_entry,
    20|    now_iso,
    21|    read_json,
    22|    slugify,
    23|    write_json,
    24|    write_text,
    25|)
    26|
    27|SECTION_DIRS = ('entities', 'concepts', 'comparisons', 'queries')
    28|
    29|
    30|def load_entries(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    31|    payload = read_json(path)
    32|    entries = payload.get('entries') if isinstance(payload, dict) else payload
    33|    if not isinstance(entries, list):
    34|        raise CompanyWikiError('Expected a top-level JSON array or an object with an entries array.')
    35|    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    36|    for item in entries:
    37|        if not isinstance(item, dict):
    38|            raise CompanyWikiError('Every entry must be a JSON object.')
    39|        ensure_entry(item)
    40|        out.append(item)
    41|    return out
    42|
    43|
    44|def classify_entry(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    45|    topic = str(entry.get('topic') or '').lower()
    46|    title = str(entry.get('title') or '').lower()
    47|    source_type = str(entry.get('source_type') or '').lower()
    48|    text = ' '.join([topic, title])
    49|    if 'comparison' in text or 'vs' in text:
    50|        return 'comparisons'
    51|    if source_type == 'trello' and topic.startswith('trello-board-'):
    52|        return 'entities'
    53|    if source_type == 'slack' and topic.startswith('public-channel-'):
    54|        return 'entities'
    55|    if 'overview' in text or topic.endswith('-overview') or 'history capture' in text:
    56|        return 'concepts'
    57|    return 'queries'
    58|
    59|
    60|def merge_entries(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    61|    grouped: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    62|    buckets: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
    63|    for entry in entries:
    64|        key = slugify(str(entry.get('topic') or entry.get('title')))
    65|        buckets[key].append(entry)
    66|
    67|    for key, group in buckets.items():
    68|        title = str(group[0].get('title') or group[0].get('topic') or key)
    69|        bucket = group[0]
    70|        section = classify_entry(bucket)
    71|        grouped[key] = {
    72|            'slug': key,
    73|            'title': title,
    74|            'section': section,
    75|            'summary': ' '.join(compact_unique(str(item.get('summary', '')) for item in group)),
    76|            'facts': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('facts', []) or []),
    77|            'decisions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('decisions', []) or []),
    78|            'actions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('actions', []) or []),
    79|            'open_questions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('open_questions', []) or []),
    80|            'entities': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('entities', []) or []),
    81|            'channel_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('channel_refs', []) or []),
    82|            'source_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('source_refs', []) or []),
    83|            'source_types': compact_unique(str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip()),
    84|            'access_scopes': compact_unique(str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip()),
    85|            'raw_manifest_refs': compact_unique(str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip()),
    86|            'time_ranges': compact_unique(str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip()),
    87|            'confidences': compact_unique(str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip()),
    88|            'updated_at': compact_unique(str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip())[-1:] or [now_iso()],
    89|            'classification_rationale': f"classified as {section} from topic/title/source_type heuristics",
    90|        }
    91|    return grouped
    92|
    93|
    94|def render_page(page: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    95|    frontmatter = [
    96|        '---',
    97|        f"title: {page['title']}",
    98|        f"updated: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
    99|        f"type: {page['section'][:-1] if page['section'].endswith('s') else page['section']}",
   100|        f"sources: [{', '.join(page['raw_manifest_refs'])}]",
   101|        '---',
   102|        '',
   103|    ]
   104|    lines = [
   105|        f"# {page['title']}",
   106|        '',
   107|        f"- Updated at: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
   108|        f"- Classification: {page['section']}",
   109|        f"- Confidence: {', '.join(page['confidences']) or 'unspecified'}",
   110|        f"- Source types: {', '.join(page['source_types']) or 'unspecified'}",
   111|        f"- Access scopes: {', '.join(page['access_scopes']) or 'unspecified'}",
   112|        f"- Provenance: {', '.join(page['raw_manifest_refs']) or 'none recorded'}",
   113|        '',
   114|        '## Summary',
   115|        page['summary'],
   116|        '',
   117|        '## Classification note',
   118|        f"- {page['classification_rationale']}",
   119|    ]
   120|    sections = [
   121|        ('Key facts', page['facts']),
   122|        ('Decisions', page['decisions']),
   123|        ('Actions', page['actions']),
   124|        ('Open questions', page['open_questions']),
   125|        ('Entities', page['entities']),
   126|        ('Channel references', page['channel_refs']),
   127|        ('Source references', page['source_refs']),
   128|        ('Raw manifest references', page['raw_manifest_refs']),
   129|    ]
   130|    for title, items in sections:
   131|        lines += ['', f'## {title}']
   132|        lines.extend(f'- {item}' for item in items) if items else lines.append('- none recorded')
   133|    lines += [
   134|        '',
   135|        '## Curation note',
   136|        '- This page was built from structured summaries only.',
   137|        '- Historical imported raw artifacts remain immutable even when they still contain old-root path strings.',
   138|    ]
   139|    return '\n'.join(frontmatter + lines) + '\n'
   140|
   141|
   142|def render_schema(root: Path) -> str:
   143|    return f"""# Company Wiki Schema
   144|
   145|## Canonical root
   146|- `{root}`
   147|
   148|## Authority
   149|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
   150|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
   151|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
   152|
   153|## Layout
   154|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
   155|- `entities/` — durable subjects
   156|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
   157|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
   158|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
   159|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
   160|
   161|## Classification fallback
   162|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
   163|"""
   164|
   165|
   166|def render_index(pages_by_section: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]) -> str:
   167|    total = sum(len(items) for items in pages_by_section.values())
   168|    lines = [
   169|        '# Company Wiki Index',
   170|        '',
   171|        f'- Built at: {now_iso()}',
   172|        f'- Page count: {total}',
   173|    ]
   174|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   175|        lines += ['', f'## {section.title()}']
   176|        items = sorted(pages_by_section.get(section, []), key=lambda item: item['title'].lower())
   177|        if not items:
   178|            lines.append('- none')
   179|            continue
   180|        for page in items:
   181|            lines.append(f"- [{page['title']}]({section}/{page['slug']}.md) — updated {page['updated_at']}")
   182|    return '\n'.join(lines) + '\n'
   183|
   184|
   185|def clean_section_dirs(root_paths: dict[str, Path]) -> None:
   186|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   187|        section_dir = root_paths[section]
   188|        if section_dir.exists():
   189|            shutil.rmtree(section_dir)
   190|        section_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   191|
   192|
   193|def main() -> None:
   194|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Build the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from structured summary JSON.')
   195|    parser.add_argument('--input', required=True, help='Path to structured summary JSON.')
   196|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT), help='Canonical company wiki root.')
   197|    args = parser.parse_args()
   198|
   199|    root = Path(args.root)
   200|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
   201|    ensure_contract_ready('company-wiki', root=root)
   202|    root_paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   203|    entries = load_entries(Path(args.input))
   204|    merged = merge_entries(entries)
   205|    clean_section_dirs(root_paths)
   206|
   207|    manifest_pages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   208|    pages_by_section: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
   209|    for slug, page in merged.items():
   210|        section_dir = root_paths[page['section']]
   211|        page_path = section_dir / f'{slug}.md'
   212|        write_text(page_path, render_page(page))
   213|        record = {
   214|            'slug': slug,
   215|            'title': page['title'],
   216|            'path': str(page_path),
   217|            'updated_at': page['updated_at'][0],
   218|            'source_types': page['source_types'],
   219|            'section': page['section'],
   220|        }
   221|        manifest_pages.append(record)
   222|        pages_by_section[page['section']].append(record)
   223|
   224|    write_text(root_paths['schema'], render_schema(root_paths['root']))
   225|    write_text(root_paths['index'], render_index(pages_by_section))
   226|    write_json(root_paths['build_manifest'], {
   227|        'built_at': now_iso(),
   228|        'entry_count': len(entries),
   229|        'page_count': len(merged),
   230|        'canonical_root': str(root_paths['root']),
   231|        'pages': manifest_pages,
   232|    })
   233|    append_contract_log(
   234|        'company-wiki',
   235|        'build',
   236|        root=root_paths['root'],
   237|        input_path=args.input,
   238|        output_root=root_paths['root'],
   239|        entry_count=len(entries),
   240|        page_count=len(merged),
   241|    )
   242|
   243|    print(json.dumps({
   244|        'ok': True,
   245|        'built_at': now_iso(),
   246|        'entry_count': len(entries),
   247|        'page_count': len(merged),
   248|        'root': str(root_paths['root']),
   249|        'manifest': str(root_paths['build_manifest']),
   250|    }, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
   251|
   252|
   253|if __name__ == '__main__':
   254|    try:
   255|        main()
   256|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   257|        raise SystemExit(str(exc))
   258|", "total_lines": 257, "file_size": 10214, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import argparse
     5|import json
     6|import shutil
     7|import subprocess
     8|import sys
     9|from pathlib import Path
    10|from typing import Any
    11|
    12|import slack_snapshot
    13|import trello_snapshot
    14|from _common import (
    15|    COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT,
    16|    LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    17|    CompanyWikiError,
    18|    append_contract_log,
    19|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root,
    20|    company_wiki_paths,
    21|    ensure_contract_ready,
    22|    now_iso,
    23|    write_json,
    24|)
    25|
    26|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
    27|TMP_ROOT = ROOT / 'workspace' / 'tmp'
    28|
    29|
    30|def copy_tree_contents(src: Path, dest: Path) -> None:
    31|    dest.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    32|    for item in src.iterdir():
    33|        target = dest / item.name
    34|        if target.exists():
    35|            continue
    36|        if item.is_dir():
    37|            shutil.copytree(item, target)
    38|        else:
    39|            shutil.copy2(item, target)
    40|
    41|
    42|def import_legacy_raw_history(raw_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    43|    imported_root = raw_root / 'imports' / 'legacy-workspace-raw-company-wiki'
    44|    result = {
    45|        'legacy_source': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    46|        'import_root': str(imported_root),
    47|        'imported': False,
    48|        'copied_item_count': 0,
    49|        'cutover_map': str(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json'),
    50|        'legacy_exists': LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists(),
    51|    }
    52|    if not LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists():
    53|        return result
    54|
    55|    copy_tree_contents(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT, imported_root)
    56|    result['imported'] = True
    57|    result['copied_item_count'] = sum(1 for _ in imported_root.rglob('*'))
    58|    cutover_map = {
    59|        'cutover_at': now_iso(),
    60|        'policy': {
    61|            'historical_artifacts_are_immutable': True,
    62|            'old_root_paths_preserved_in_historical_artifacts': True,
    63|            'silent_path_rewrite_for_raw_evidence': False,
    64|            'canonical_provenance_exposed_via_cutover_mapping': True,
    65|        },
    66|        'legacy_source_root': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    67|        'canonical_import_root': str(imported_root),
    68|    }
    69|    write_json(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json', cutover_map)
    70|    append_contract_log(
    71|        'company-wiki',
    72|        'raw-import',
    73|        root=raw_root.parent,
    74|        legacy_source=LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    75|        import_root=imported_root,
    76|    )
    77|    return result
    78|
    79|
    80|def main() -> None:
    81|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Refresh the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from Slack and Trello snapshots.')

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import argparse
     5|import json
     6|import re
     7|from pathlib import Path
     8|
     9|from _common import COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT, CompanyWikiError
    10|
    11|SECTION_DIRS = ('entities', 'concepts', 'comparisons', 'queries')
    12|
    13|
    14|def tokenize(value: str) -> list[str]:
    15|    return [token for token in re.findall(r'[a-zA-Z0-9가-힣_-]+', value.lower()) if token]
    16|
    17|
    18|def load_pages(root: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    19|    pages: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    20|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
    21|        section_dir = root / section
    22|        if not section_dir.exists():
    23|            continue
    24|        for path in sorted(section_dir.glob('*.md')):
    25|            pages.append({
    26|                'path': str(path),
    27|                'name': path.stem,
    28|                'section': section,
    29|                'text': path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'),
    30|            })
    31|    if not pages:
    32|        raise CompanyWikiError(f'No company wiki section pages found under {root}')
    33|    return pages
    34|
    35|
    36|def score_page(page: dict[str, str], query_tokens: list[str]) -> tuple[int, list[str]]:
    37|    text_lower = page['text'].lower()
    38|    name_lower = page['name'].lower()
    39|    section_lower = page['section'].lower()
    40|    score = 0
    41|    snippets: list[str] = []
    42|    for token in query_tokens:
    43|        score += name_lower.count(token) * 5
    44|        score += section_lower.count(token) * 3
    45|        score += text_lower.count(token)
    46|    for line in page['text'].splitlines():
    47|        lowered = line.lower()
    48|        if any(token in lowered for token in query_tokens):
    49|            snippets.append(line.strip())
    50|        if len(snippets) >= 3:
    51|            break
    52|    return score, snippets
    53|
    54|
    55|def render_markdown(results: list[dict[str, object]], query: str) -> str:
    56|    lines = [
    57|        '# Company Wiki query results',
    58|        '',
    59|        f'- Query: `{query}`',
    60|        f'- Matches: {len(results)}',
    61|        '',
    62|        '## Results',
    63|    ]
    64|    for result in results:
    65|        lines.append(f"- `{result['path']}` — section={result['section']} score={result['score']}")
    66|        for snippet in result['snippets']:
    67|            lines.append(f'  - {snippet}')
    68|    return '\n'.join(lines)
    69|
    70|
    71|def main() -> None:
    72|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Query the canonical Hermes-style company wiki.')
    73|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT))
    74|    parser.add_argument('--query', required=True)
    75|    parser.add_argument('--limit', type=int, default=5)
    76|    parser.add_argument('--format', choices=['markdown', 'json'], default='markdown')
    77|    args = parser.parse_args()
    78|
    79|    query_tokens = tokenize(args.query)
    80|    if not query_tokens:
    81|        raise CompanyWikiError('Query must contain at least one searchable token.')
    82|
    83|    scored = []
    84|    for page in load_pages(Path(args.root)):
    85|        score, snippets = score_page(page, query_tokens)
    86|        if score > 0:
    87|            scored.append({
    88|                'path': page['path'],
    89|                'section': page['section'],
    90|                'score': score,
    91|                'snippets': snippets,
    92|            })
    93|    scored.sort(key=lambda item: (-int(item['score']), str(item['path'])))
    94|    results = scored[: max(1, args.limit)]
    95|
    96|    if args.format == 'json':
    97|        print(json.dumps({'query': args.query, 'results': results}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
    98|    else:
    99|        print(render_markdown(results, args.query))
   100|
   101|
   102|if __name__ == '__main__':
   103|    try:
   104|        main()
   105|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   106|        raise SystemExit(str(exc))
   107|", "total_lines": 106, "file_size": 3472, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

messages.reasoning:
**Evaluating wiki structure**

I'm considering how to manage the wiki's structure since immediate results are important. I think it’s better to patch the builder for order and bugs first, and then create a query page after that. If we keep manual pages separate from generated ones, we could use a new directory for them. However, since the querying tool only scans section directories, there might be some limitations unless we adjust the SECTION_DIRS or use raw imports.
**Improving index generation**

I'm realizing that index generation only uses pages by section. I could patch the render_index to include a static "Landing guide" block with available sources from the repo, eliminating the need for individual pages. This should help with the initial wiki reorder and ground everything in retrieved evidence. I also need to patch render_schema to define source coverage and reading order, while fixing the `type: entity` bug. This approach feels like a good sustainable change.
**Enhancing render_index functionality**

I can also patch render_index to include a "## Start Here" section with the current recommended reading order. This would list queries, concepts, and entities in that order. Additionally, I think I can add a "## Source surface map" bullet list to link resources like Slack, Trello, and GitHub without needing extra pages. I should also patch render_schema to include source coverage and maintain proper reading order. Lastly, maybe I can consider sorting queries by updated dates instead of alphabetically.
**Implementing query highlights**

I think the execution layer should really focus on highlighting the latest query. It might be effective to implement a clear order for this. By emphasizing the most recent queries, I can help streamline the user's experience and make it easier for them to find what they need quickly. This change could enhance overall efficiency and engagement with the system. Let's look into how best to implement that!
**Planning the patch**

I’m thinking about ways to refine the patch plan. First, I might classify some resource map entries as unnecessary. I need to fix the type line, probably with a singular type mapping for easier handling of special-case entities. For the render schema, I'll add sections for reading order and coverage model. It’s crucial to detail which sources feed into the structure, and it might be beneficial to include an introduction with counts per section, dynamically created. Let’s move ahead with this implementation!
**Developing sorting logic**

I’m crafting a pseudo-function to sort sections based on specific criteria. For the 'queries' section, I think I'll sort primarily by the updated date in descending order, and then by title. For the 'concepts' section, it might make sense to prioritize items according to keywords in their titles. This approach should help keep the content organized and accessible for users. Let’s refine these sorting methods to ensure they meet expectations effectively!
**Considering log updates**

I'm thinking about updating the log with "manual-reorder" and an evidence summary. However, the log generated by the build appends the contract build event only. I wonder if I can manually append something instead, like a log entry after a rebuild. Creating a manual page for the query could also work, but I need to account for future overwrites. It seems a note in the log might be the best immediate solution, especially since the user requested initial wiki reordering.
**Exploring evidence note creation**

I'm considering whether I can create a raw evidence note under _build. I should verify if the build uses the latest entries file and if a refresh is necessary. After patching, I could rebuild from the most recent normalized entries. I also want to think about patching the index sort. For that, it looks like I need to edit wiki_build.py and utilize a patch. Let's see how all of this should come together!

{"mode":"replace","path":"workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py","old_string":"def render_schema(root: Path) -> str:
    return f"""# Company Wiki Schema

## Canonical root
- `{root}`

## Authority
- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.

    return f"""# Company Wiki Schema

## Canonical root
- `{root}`

## Authority
- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.

## Reading order
1. `queries/` — latest executive memo, realignment note, or retained synthesis first
2. `concepts/` — source-overview pages that explain what Slack/Trello signals currently mean
3. `entities/` — channel, board, repo, or other subject-level drill-down only after the executive/context pages
4. `comparisons/` — explicit tradeoff pages when multiple options or surfaces are being evaluated side-by-side

## Coverage model
- Auto-built canonical pages currently come from normalized Slack + Trello snapshots.
- GitHub, Orbi MCP, repo skills/docs, and cron/runtime state are valid orientation sources for manual wiki reordering and audits, even when they are not yet auto-ingested into normalized entries.
- Derived pages must keep provenance to manifests, source refs, or clearly named live evidence.
- Raw source bodies stay under `raw/`; derived pages should stay summary-first.

## Layout
- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
- `entities/` — durable subjects
- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata

## Classification fallback
- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
"""
"}

+++ b/workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py
@@ -248,6 +248,34 @@
         section_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 
 
+def frontmatter_field(text: str, field: str) -> str:
+    match = re.search(rf'^{re.escape(field)}:\s*(.+)$', text, re.MULTILINE)
+    return match.group(1).strip() if match else ''
+
+
+def load_preserved_pages(root_paths: dict[str, Path], generated_slugs: set[str]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
+    preserved: list[dict[str, str]] = []
+    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
+        section_dir = root_paths[section]
+        if not section_dir.exists():
+            continue
+        for path in sorted(section_dir.glob('*.md')):
+            slug = path.stem
+            if slug in generated_slugs:
+                continue
+            text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
+            title = frontmatter_field(text, 'title') or slug.replace('-', ' ')
+            updated_at = frontmatter_field(text, 'updated') or now_iso()
+            preserved.append({
+                'slug': slug,
+                'title': title,
+                'section': section,
+                'updated_at': updated_at,
+                'text': text,
+            })
+    return preserved
+
+
 def main() -> None:
     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Build the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from structured summary JSON.')
     parser.add_argument('--input', required=True, help='Path to structured summary JSON.')

---

# 2026-04-13 initial wiki reordering

## Situation
초기 위키는 Slack/Trello 수집은 붙어 있는데, 첫 화면이 아직 **엔티티 나열형**이다. 운영 리드가 바로 읽어야 할 순서는 엔티티가 아니라 **판단 메모 → 소스 개요 → 개별 대상**이다.

## Evidence snapshot
- Slack 최신 run manifest 기준 public channels 7개 join 성공, history capture 성공 7개, chunk 저장은 `#서비스-배포-알림`, `#ai_news`에서 확인된다.
- Trello 최신 manifest 기준 visible workspace 1개, open boards 4개, collection_count 6이다.
- GitHub live surface는 정상 인증돼 있고 최근 업데이트 상위 repo는 `orbi-oracle-bot`, `ai-math`, `tutor`, `orbi-viewer-mcp`, `spica` 순으로 보인다.
- Orbi live surface는 hot posts와 trending searches가 현재 응답한다. 반면 MCP generic list endpoints는 이 브리지에서 직접 노출되지 않았다.
- 시스템 crontab에는 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있다.
- skill/router 기준 회사 위키 해석면은 `company-wiki`, Slack 수집 lane은 `slack-org-wiki`, GitHub 실행면은 `github-org-intel`, Trello exec 해석면은 `trello-wiki-exec-report`, 자동화 문맥은 `create_slack_cron`이 담당한다.

## Analysis
### 1. 첫 화면은 executive-first가 맞다
대표/운영 리드가 처음 봐야 하는 건 “무슨 소스가 살아 있고”, “무엇이 자동 반영되고”, “어디까지가 아직 수동 해석 구간인지”다.

### 2. canonical auto-ingest와 live-access surface를 분리해서 보여줘야 한다
현재 canonical auto-ingest는 Slack+Trello다. 하지만 실제 운영상 사용 가능한 리소스는 GitHub, Orbi, skill/docs, cron까지 넓다. 이 차이를 인덱스와 스키마에서 명시해야 오해가 줄어든다.

### 3. 위키 hygiene는 바로잡아야 했다
빌더가 `entities -> entitie`로 잘못 단수화하고 있었고, 인덱스도 단순 section dump였다. 이건 검색 품질과 신뢰를 같이 깎는다.

## Reordered reading path
1. `queries/` — 최신 판단 메모
2. `concepts/` — Slack/Trello source overview
3. `entities/` — channel/board drill-down
4. `comparisons/` — 선택지 비교가 생길 때 추가

## Recommendation
- company wiki의 기본 독서 순서를 executive-first로 고정한다.
- 인덱스에 Slack/Trello auto-ingest, GitHub/Orbi live surface, skill/docs/cron context를 분리 표기한다.
- 수동 판단 메모는 보존 대상으로 취급해서 다음 빌드에도 살아남게 해야 한다.

## Result applied in this cycle
- `wiki_build.py`에서 `type: entity` 단수화 버그를 수정했다.
- `SCHEMA.md`에 reading order와 coverage model을 추가했다.
- `index.md`를 executive-first landing 구조로 재작성했다.
- 수동 query/comparison 페이지가 빌드 시 사라지지 않도록 preservation 로직을 추가했다.
"}

  - `queries → concepts → entities` 순서로 읽도록 고정

  - 초기 위키 재정렬 메모를 새로 추가
  - Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi/skills/cron 근거를 모두 묶어 둠
- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py`
  - `type: entitie` 버그 수정 → `type: entity`
  - 단순 section dump 인덱스 → **executive-first index generator**로 변경
  - 수동 `query/comparison` 페이지가 다음 빌드 때 **안 날아가게 preservation 로직** 추가

검증:
- 빌드 재실행 성공

  - 시작점이 `2026-04-13 initial wiki reordering`로 잡힘
- 쿼리 검증
  - `python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py --root workspace/company-wiki --query "initial wiki reordering github orbi cron"`
  - 새 query 페이지가 1순위로 검색됨
- entity 타입 검증
  - `workspace/company-wiki/entities/public-channel-c09dud9amfa-ai-orbi.md:4` → `type: entity`

이번 재정렬에 실제로 반영한 리소스:
- Slack: `raw/run-manifests/20260413T091942Z.json`, `raw/slack/runs/manifest-20260413T091943Z.json`
- Trello: `raw/trello/runs/manifest-20260413T092009Z.json`
- GitHub live: `gh auth status`, `gh repo list orbisoptimus --limit 20`

남은 구조적 갭 1개만 짚으면:
- **GitHub/Orbi는 아직 canonical normalized entries로 auto-ingest되진 않는다.**
- 지금은 인덱스/스키마/판단 메모에서 “live available surface”로 명확히 올려놨다. 즉, 위키 첫 화면은 정리됐고, 다음 단계는 **GitHub/Orbi를 실제 entry generator에 합류시키는 것**이다.
